So if I go into the schema.org here what Facebook are collecting data on is not just things like the URL, the image, etc. etc. but they're collecting a lot more as well and giving more context to Facebook like what kind of product is this what kind of category really to figure out what kind of person would be buying this and Facebook are using that data to customize experience and this is where it comes back to cold audiences where often and this is a recommendation for Facebook especially for cold audiences dynamic product ads often work with really really broad audiences so for example I'm running this campaign at the moment and if I just click into edit I'm doing a test at the moment on really kind of low-level testing I've got my product set which is part of the overall catalog and what I'm doing here is to test the broad audiences against the whole of the US so this website's been going for about six seven months it's a passionate niche we've collected lots of data the pixels been collecting lots of data and sending back we've got purchase data so Facebook has a lot to play with and essentially what I'm trying to do is to get Facebook to present the right products to the right users so it's a really kind of super broad and targeting which you can see here and if I go into the ad itself the ads are collection ads so I'm running collection ads which if you've seen the collection ad training is really really easy to set up you have your copy you can drop a video or you can kind of let Facebook create one for you and then Facebook will personalize the image of the products that presented below and it just creates a more immersive experience for for the user and keeps them
